rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU VS. SHUZHEN TU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Declaring Vi Dan Tran A Vexatious Litigant And For Security
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 15, 2024 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT BRYANT FU, CRYSTAL LEI Motion For Order Declaring Vi Dan Tran A Vexatious Litigant And For Security is transferred to department 302 to be heard on August 29, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. per the order of the presiding judge. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HARRY HUEY VS. ROBERT JOHNSTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Robert Johnston; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 15, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF HARRY HUEY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Robert Johnston; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities is GRANTED in part and moot in part. Motion is moot as to request for admissions and responses have been provided. Motion is otherwise granted. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections by 5:00 p.m. on August 16, 2024. If defendant fails to provide responses as herein ordered, sanctions are granted in the amount of $1,512. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
